function.name: observe_image
function.description: Detect and persist observations for every entity (face) in an image. Accepts a base64-encoded image, runs the detector + encoder, and returns the observation ids + bboxes that can feed into `label`. Idempotent: re-observing the same image returns the same ids.

image_base64.description: Raw image bytes, base64-encoded (standard or URL-safe). The server decodes, hashes, runs the detector, and persists one observation per detected face.

source_uri.description: Caller's reference to the image (path, URL, or opaque id). The library stores this but does not fetch it.

function.name: identify_image
function.description: Identify entities in an image without mutating state. Runs the detector + encoder and returns ranked Candidate matches per detected face.

image_base64.description: Raw image bytes, base64-encoded (standard or URL-safe). The server decodes, hashes, runs the detector, and persists one observation per detected face.

k.description: Max number of candidates to return per face.

min_confidence.description: Drop candidates below this similarity threshold. Defaults are conservative; tune up for stricter matching.

prefer.description: Ranking preference when both instance and type entities match the same observation.

function.name: identify_by_name
function.description: Resolve an entity by name or id and return its recall snapshot (entity metadata + active facts). Convenience wrapper around `recall`.

entity_name_or_id.description: Either an entity id (ent_*) or a human-readable name / alias. Ids take precedence; name lookup falls back to exact-match on name or alias.

function.name: label
function.description: Commit a user-authoritative positive binding: 'these observations are this entity'. Creates the entity if the name is new. Use to teach.

observation_ids.description: Observations to bind to the target entity. Typically returned by a prior `observe_image` call.

entity_name_or_id.description: Target entity — id (ent_*) to reuse, or a new name to create an entity. Re-using a name never triggers a merge; identity is the id, not the name.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: relabel
function.description: Move a single observation to a different entity. Also emits a negative binding against the old entity so the auto-clusterer never re-attaches.

new_entity_name_or_id.description: Target entity for the observation. Same name/id rules as `label`.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: merge
function.description: 'These are the same.' Folds losers into a winner entity; facts and relationships migrate with provenance. Rejects modality or kind mismatches. `keep` is 'oldest' (default) or an explicit entity id.

entity_ids.description: Entities to merge. Requires at least two. All must share the same modality and kind.

keep.description: Winner selection: 'oldest' picks the one with the earliest `created_at`; otherwise pass an entity id to force that entity to win.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: split
function.description: 'This is actually N different entities.' `groups[0]` stays on the original id; each subsequent group becomes a new entity. Cross-wise negative bindings prevent auto-re-merge. `fact_policy` controls whether facts are copied, kept on the original, or deferred to manual reconciliation.

groups.description: List of observation-id groups. `groups[0]` stays on the original entity; each subsequent group becomes a new entity with the same kind + modality.

fact_policy.description: How to treat facts on the original entity. `keep_original` (default) leaves them on the original; `copy_to_all` duplicates them to each split-off entity; `manual` defers to the caller.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: forget
function.description: Hard-delete everything tied to an entity and prune old LanceDB versions (GDPR Art. 17). NOT reversible by undo. Returns per-table deletion counts.

grace_days.description: Reserved for a future soft-delete grace window. v0 always hard-deletes regardless of this value.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: restrict
function.description: Stop using the entity for inference without deleting it (GDPR Art. 18). Restricted entities are excluded from `identify_image` but their facts remain readable via `recall`.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: unrestrict
function.description: Reverse `restrict` — return the entity to ACTIVE status.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: remember
function.description: Attach a free-text fact to an entity. Facts are bi-temporal and stay valid until retracted.

content.description: Natural-language fact. The library stores this as opaque text; callers that need structure can embed JSON.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: recall
function.description: Return an entity plus its currently-active facts.

function.name: undo
function.description: Reverse a prior reversible operation. With no `event_id`, undoes the most recent reversible event by `actor`. `forget` is not reversible by undo.

event_id.description: EventLog id of the event to reverse. Omit to undo the most recent reversible event by the calling actor.

actor.description: Who is making this call, for audit attribution. Convention: "kind:id" — e.g. "user:alice", "agent:my_assistant", "llm:gpt-4". Defaults to "mcp:unknown" when omitted.

function.name: export
function.description: GDPR Art. 20 data portability dump — observations, bindings, facts, events, relationships, event log for one entity. Raw embedding vectors are excluded by default (biometric vectors in exports are usually worse than useless).

include_embeddings.description: Include raw embedding vectors in the dump. Off by default; turn on only for round-trip migration.
